item.title: GADTs in Haskell
item.description: Generalized Algebraic Data Types (GADTs) generalizes ordinary algebraic data types by permitting value constructors to return specific types. GADTs are used for ensuring program correctness and in generic programming. This article is specific to Haskell programming language. In Haskell GADTs are implemented as a language extension. The article describes these use cases with small programs. Introduction Type system help programmers ensure that the software they write behave correctly. They detect errors and also serve as documentation.
